pending_context.whisper: "St. Benedict the Moor Catholic School" - Black Catholic school

pending_context.notes: Pass 1 row 5.3 captures speaker's reference. The school was founded by the Josephite Fathers in the early 20th century for Black Catholic education in St. Augustine; canonical local institution. Speaker-originating is correct categorization but worth noting as a cross-entry reference if other St. Augustine speakers also reference it.

pending_context.candidate_correction: likely "Mr. Warren" (production-crew member) OR scratch-take fragment
pending_context.notes: Appears in segment 2 of SRT at 00:00:09-00:00:12, between Mosnier's LoC/Smithsonian intro and "Okay. We're all in." Likely a stray reference to a production-crew member (sound tech or assistant). The entry #4 (Hamilton & Ulmer) recorded same day also has a "Warren" reference. Flag for adversarial review.

"thirty eight, thirty nine from the Royal School" — Pass 1+2+3 didn't flag this. Royal Memorial School (a/k/a Royal School) was an established Black grade school in St. Augustine pre-integration, distinct from Excelsior High (the high school named in Pass 1 row 5.2). Vickers is naming her grade-school years (1938-39) vs. her high-school graduation (1942). Flag for adversarial review on the canonical name.

"I went to the wooden letters in the church. So the two young men, young white fellows..." — context suggests the speaker is describing the white people at the church who refused her entry (the kneel-in protocol). "Wooden letters" is unrecoverable but plausibly "white ushers" or "the white folks." Flag for adversarial review.

"they shot Dr. Stolz, Madonna, and the boxer" — "Dr. Stolz" is Whisper's rendering of the possessive "Doctor's" (i.e., Dr. Hayling's dogs). Pass 1 row 5.11 caught "Madonna" as the dog name but did not catch "Dr. Stolz" as a token-merge of "Doctor's." Cross-reference with the historical record: the September 1963 Klan attack on Hayling's home killed his dog(s).

pending_context.whisper: "red boat you had him at the top"
pending_context.notes: "He came to pick me up. And do you know? He did not go home. He went downtown in a red boat you had him at the top going back." — Whisper failure on whatever Vickers said about Hayling's late-night downtown deposit-drop. The "red boat" is recoverable as "red [garment]" given the comedic incongruity Vickers is establishing (Hayling driving downtown in his bathrobe). Flag for adversarial review.

"They have their peers and their garden clothes and listen to it." — Whisper failure. Likely "garments" or "households" given context of white people who couldn't be openly supportive of Black neighbors. Flag for adversarial review.

"I went to Fort Lauderdale one time it was during the Christmas. And Grin said, that's Miss Haling." Recurring proper-noun "Grin" — could be a person's name (e.g., "Glenn") or a family relationship ("granddaughter"). Pass 1+2+3 missed. Flag for adversarial review.

Vickers's father worked as maître d' at "the Bennett Hotel" a few doors down from the Monson. Real St. Augustine hotel (Bennett House / Bennett Hotel was a documented early-20th-century property). Pass 1+2+3 didn't itemize; speaker-originating but worth noting for catalog.

Coherent with Pass 1 row 5.11 (Madonna = boxer dog). No correction.
"It wasn't the city, it was the Nielins, the sleeping, swimming." See 5.P4.4 above; reinforce as a missed enumeration of direct-action tactics.
Hayling's hand-wave moment at the monument unveiling, after Vickers spoke. Whisper-token uncertain. Speaker-originating phrasing; minor.

"He had friends. And I guess he thought the Aster's with the keep his children. And until he could take them down to Tallahassee." — possibly a friend or family name "Astor" or similar; unrecoverable. Flag for adversarial review.
